description: Lookup table for classifying codes as administrative vs. medical/pharmacy. Codes listed here will be filtered out during Feature Importance EDA feature filtering.
documentation: See 3a_feature_importance/README.md for details on Z code filtering. These 4 Z codes (Z00.00, Z00.01, Z00.121, Z00.129) were identified as administrative based on feature importance analysis showing they represent routine administrative examinations rather than clinical risk factors.

notes.icd: ICD codes for administrative/billing purposes (e.g., routine health checks, administrative visits)
notes.cpt: CPT codes for administrative procedures (e.g., post-operative follow-up, administrative services)
notes.drug: Drug codes that are administrative (e.g., placebo, administrative medications). Usually empty - most drugs are clinical.

how_to_identify_administrative_codes: 1. Review code_analysis_protocol_vs_clinical CSV from research outputs
2. Identify codes with high protocol_pct (>80%) that appear in protocol-like sequences
3. Manually review codes to determine if they are administrative vs. clinical
4. Add confirmed administrative codes to this lookup table
5. Codes should be administrative/billing/scheduling, not clinical diagnoses or treatments
research_guidelines.post_event_leakage: Events occurring on or after target event date are automatically classified as administrative (leakage) and do not need to be in this lookup table.
